title: cerastium semidecandrum subsp. dentatum
intro: Cerastium semidecandrum subsp. dentatum is a plant species, also known as the toothed mouse-ear, which belongs to the carnation family and is native to certain regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The toothed mouse-ear plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, as well as supporting digestive health. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to make teas or tonics for medicinal purposes.
